clinical_history: 70 year old woman with ECG showing which of the following major diagnoses?
diagnosis: The ECG shows a narrow complex tachycardia at an average ventricular rate of about 150/min. The cadence is predominantly very regular with occasional exceptions. The atrial morphology at a constant rate of about 300 cycles/min is that of typical atrial flutter, with predominant 2:1 AV conduction and some variable block. The monotonous consistency and appearance of the atrial waves and the preciseness of the R-R patterns indicates atrial flutter, not fibrillation. Q waves with ST elevations indicate an MI that is likely acute/evolving involving antero-apical/lateral and inferior segments. Relatively low limb and lateral precordial QRS voltage is present. The patient had underlying severe coronary artery disease.
